AdministrativeStatus.description: The status of the endpoint. If the endpoint is Enabled, it is probed for endpoint health and is included in the traffic routing method.

AlwaysServe.description: If Always Serve is enabled, probing for endpoint health will be disabled and endpoints will be included in the traffic routing method.

Azure.ResourceManager.CommonTypes.Tracke.title: Tracked Resource
Azure.ResourceManager.CommonTypes.Tracke.description: The resource model definition for an Azure Resource Manager tracked top level resource which has 'tags' and a 'location'

CustomTopologyMap.description: The experience level of the Private Traffic Manager profile.

DnsConfig.description: DNS configuration of the Traffic Manager profile.

recordType.description: The record type of the Traffic Manager profile.

ttl.description: The TTL of the DNS records in seconds.

Endpoint.description: Class representing a Private Traffic Manager endpoint.

properties.description: The properties of the Private Traffic Manager endpoint.

EndpointListResult.description: The response of a Endpoint list operation.

value.description: The Endpoint items on this page

nextLink.description: The link to the next page of items

EndpointProperties.description: Class representing a Traffic Manager endpoint properties.

target.description: The fully-qualified DNS name or IP address of the endpoint. Traffic Manager returns this value in DNS responses to direct traffic to this endpoint.

monitoringTarget.description: Monitoring target is where Private Traffic Manager will gather health information.If MonitoringTarget is not configured EndpointTarget will be used instead. If the EndpointTarget is IPv6 then the Monitoring Target MUST be configured. Monitoring Target cannot be an IPv6 address.

endpointStatus.description: The status of the endpoint. If the endpoint is Enabled, it is probed for endpoint health and is included in the traffic routing method.

kind.description: Metadata used by portal/tooling/etc to render different UX experiences for resources of the same type; e.g. ApiApps are a kind of Microsoft.Web/sites type.  If supported, the resource provider must validate and persist this value.

weight.description: The weight of this endpoint when using the 'Weighted' traffic routing method. Possible values are from 1 to 1000.

priority.description: The priority of this endpoint when using the 'Priority' traffic routing method. Possible values are from 1 to 1000, lower values represent higher priority. This is an optional parameter.  If specified, it must be specified on all endpoints, and no two endpoints can share the same priority value.

alwaysServe.description: Indicates whether endpoint is Always Serve or not. Always Serve endpoints are always considered to be healthy.

healthPolicyId.description: The health policy associated with this endpoint.

PrivateTrafficManagerProfileUpdate.description: Class representing a Traffic Manager profile.

properties.description: The properties of the Traffic Manager profile.

ProbeConfig.description: Class containing endpoint monitoring settings in a Traffic Manager profile.

protocol.description: The protocol (HTTP, HTTPS or TCP) used to probe for endpoint health.

port.description: The TCP port used to probe for endpoint health.

path.description: The path relative to the endpoint domain name used to probe for endpoint health.

intervalInSeconds.description: The monitor interval for endpoints in this profile. This is the interval at which Traffic Manager will check the health of each endpoint in this profile. Allowed values: 5, 10, or 30 seconds.

timeoutInSeconds.description: The monitor timeout for endpoints in this profile. This is the time that Traffic Manager allows endpoints in this profile to response to the health check.

toleratedNumberOfFailures.description: The number of consecutive failed health check that Traffic Manager tolerates before declaring an endpoint in this profile Degraded after the next failed health check.

expectedStatusCodeRanges.description: List of expected status code ranges.

ProbeHealthPolicy.description: Class representing a Traffic Manager health policy of kind Probe.

properties.description: The health policy for Traffic Manager profile properties.

ProbeHealthPolicyProperties.description: Class representing a Traffic Manager health policy of kind Probe.

name.description: The name of the health policy.

probeConfig.description: Probe monitoring settings of the Traffic Manager profile.

ProbingGatewayId.description: A type definition that refers the id to an Azure Resource Manager resource.

ProfileEndpoint.description: Class representing an inline endpoint within a Traffic Manager profile.

target.description: The fully-qualified DNS name or IP address of the endpoint. Traffic Manager returns this value in DNS responses to direct traffic to this endpoint.

monitoringTarget.description: Monitoring target is where Private Traffic Manager will gather health information.If MonitoringTarget is not configured EndpointTarget will be used instead. If the EndpointTarget is IPv6 then the Monitoring Target MUST be configured. Monitoring Target cannot be an IPv6 address.

endpointStatus.description: The status of the endpoint. If the endpoint is Enabled, it is probed for endpoint health and is included in the traffic routing method.

kind.description: Metadata used by portal/tooling/etc to render different UX experiences for resources of the same type; e.g. ApiApps are a kind of Microsoft.Web/sites type.  If supported, the resource provider must validate and persist this value.

weight.description: The weight of this endpoint when using the 'Weighted' traffic routing method. Possible values are from 1 to 1000.

priority.description: The priority of this endpoint when using the 'Priority' traffic routing method. Possible values are from 1 to 1000, lower values represent higher priority. This is an optional parameter.  If specified, it must be specified on all endpoints, and no two endpoints can share the same priority value.

alwaysServe.description: Indicates whether endpoint is Always Serve or not. Always Serve endpoints are always considered to be healthy.

healthPolicyId.description: The health policy associated with this endpoint.
